title: Managing disaster-related fiscal risks in a shock-prone world: Insights from the Cat DDO (Catastrophe Deferred Drawdown Option) experience
content: This evaluation addresses gaps related to the catastrophe deferred drawdown option (Cat DDO). espite its growing adoption, empirical evidence on the Cat DDO’s effectiveness remains limited, particularly regarding its role in post-disaster financing.
snippet: This evaluation addresses gaps related to the catastrophe deferred drawdown option (Cat DDO). espite its growing adoption, empirical evidence on the Cat DDO’s effectiveness remains limited,

title: Emergence of new heat stress hotspots over West Africa
content: Motivated by the recent record-breaking heatwaves over western Africa, the authors analyzed 50 years of hourly wet-bulb globe temperature (WBGT) data to identify emerging heatwave hotspots, characterize their timing and types, and evaluate whether simpler heatwave metrics, more commonly used by meteorological agencies, can be employed to estimate dangerous WBGT conditions. They find that exposure to WBGT ≥30 °C and <33 °C (conditions dangerous during light physical activity) has been increasing across almost all of western Africa, at rates of 30–100 h per decade.
snippet: Motivated by the recent record-breaking heatwaves over western Africa, the authors analyzed 50 years of hourly wet-bulb globe temperature (WBGT) data to identify emerging heatwave hotspots,

title: Model maps solar storms across 1 million miles around Earth
content: A team at the Applied Physics Lab is working to understand the complex science behind predicting invisible threats that can quickly cripple electric grid infrastructure on Earth.
snippet: A team at the Applied Physics Lab is working to understand the complex science behind predicting invisible threats that can quickly cripple electric grid infrastructure on Earth.

title: Report outlines potential to predict coastal cliff collapses
content: Scripps Oceanography-led research effort detected signs of cliff failure hours to days before collapse.

title: Workers are risking dangerous heat to keep the World Cup running
content: A patchwork of government protections and company policies have left them more vulnerable.

title: The oceans are full of heat, and it’s coming ashore
content: The ocean absorbs 90 percent of the excess heat from global warming. It doesn't all stay there.

title: WMO highlights sand and dust storm hotspots, impacts and research

title: Compound multi-hazard assessment under CMIP6 climate scenarios: Tracking seasonal flood-landslide and drought-fire interactions across Nepal

title: Memory sources shape flood risk perception and agricultural decision among farmers in a drought-oriented irrigation district

title: San Andreas Fault: Hidden movements revealed by artificial intelligence
content: A research team has uncovered a previously hidden population of so-called slow slip events beneath the Parkfield section of California’s San Andreas Fault.
snippet: A research team has uncovered a previously hidden population of so-called slow slip events beneath the Parkfield section of California’s San Andreas Fault.

title: From simulation to resilience: using the Stop Disasters game and local data to teach disaster risk reduction
content: No simulation can fully represent the complexity of real disasters. Stop Disasters simplifies reality. However, when combined with local hazard information and a structured learning approach, it provides students with a safe environment to make decisions.

title: Becoming a climate champion – UN CC:e-Learn course

title: Extreme weather resilience: why it matters and how we’re building it

title: Could geoengineering work to tamp down Super El Niños?
content: A 2019 Australian bushfire provided a rare opportunity to scientists by creating an effect analogous to what could be achieved through the geoengineering technique known as marine cloud brightening.

title: Building codes are not enough: Lessons from Venezuela’s earthquake

title: From fire to resilience: rethinking how Algeria manages its forests

title: How 65 major U.S. cities are responding to urban heat
content: Climate Central analysis shows that residents of the 65 largest U.S. cities experience an average of 8°F of extra heat due to the built environment.
snippet: Climate Central analysis shows that residents of the 65 largest U.S. cities experience an average of 8°F of extra heat due to the built environment.

title: Enhancing urban traffic resilience under pluvial flooding through adaptive signal control
content: This study developed an integrated simulation framework that couples hydrodynamic inundation modeling with an agent-based traffic-rescue simulation to examine adaptive resilience in urban traffic networks under compound flood-traffic conditions.

title: Early warning systems and anticipatory action: the fiscal case for pre-disaster financing

title: The complex links between food security, migration, and fragility in Northern Central America

title: Malta: Rapid emergency response and readiness assessment

title: Adapting to climate-related health risks: the economic case for climate services for health
content: This exploratory analysis estimates the economic value of “climate services for health”, defined as producing and applying climate knowledge to enhance health decisions and services.
snippet: This exploratory analysis estimates the economic value of “climate services for health”, defined as producing and applying climate knowledge to enhance health decisions and services.
